channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Major News Updates Across Nigeria – March 14, 2026
item.description: The Naija News roundup for March 14, 2026, highlight plenty happenings across politics, economy, and society. A journalist, Segun Olawoye, raised alarm say his life dey in danger, calling out Minister...

item.title: Dozens of UK MPs and Peers Urge Government to Apologise for British Mandate Actions in Palestine (1917-1948)
item.description: A cross-party group of 45 MPs and peers has signed an open letter urging the British government to issue a formal apology for its historical actions during the administration of Palestine from 1917 to...

item.title: Kenya Sees Steady Decline in Crime Over Two Years, Says Police Chief
item.description: Kenya has witnessed a significant reduction in reported criminal cases over the last two years, according to the Inspector General of the National Police Service, Douglas Kanja. He revealed that crime...

item.title: Jack White Clarifies His Comments on Taylor Swift's Songwriting Style
item.description: Rock and Roll Hall of Famer Jack White recently addressed controversy stemming from comments he made about Taylor Swift's songwriting style. In an interview with The Guardian, White expressed that he ...

item.title: Construction of Mamire Gate at Tarangire National Park Nears Completion, Promises Better Tourist Access
item.description: The Mamire entrance gate at Tarangire National Park is almost finished and it is expected to make life easier for tourists who want to visit the park through Babati District. This new gate will give m...

item.title: Civil society urges Marcos to strengthen leadership of Bangsamoro peace panel
item.description: A coalition of civil society organizations, academic institutions, and peace advocates has formally appealed to President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. to reconstitute the leadership of the Government Peace Im...

item.title: French Soldiers Injured in Drone Attack on Kurdish Base in Northern Iraq
item.description: On the night between Thursday and Friday, Sub-Officer Arnaud Frion of France's 7th Battalion of Alpine Hunters was killed in a drone attack on a joint French-Kurdish base in Mala Qara, located about 4...

item.title: PMD Warns of Rain, Snow and Storms in Northern Pakistan This Weekend
item.description: The Pakistan Meteorological Department (PMD) has forecast another round of rainfall and snowfall over northern Pakistan starting from Saturday night through to Monday morning. A westerly weather syste...

item.title: UN warns of escalating displacement crisis amid Lebanon-Israel conflict
item.description: Lebanon is facing one of the most severe waves of displacement in its recent history, with over 816,000 people registered as displaced, nearly a third of them children. The current crisis has escalate...

item.title: Woman Fabricates Husband's Death to Solicit Funds, Story Exposed
item.description: Amina Issah made headlines after she allegedly lied on live radio, claiming her husband had died in a car accident to solicit sympathy and financial support from listeners. She shared a story about be...

item.title: Tanzania to Host Key Youth Football Tournaments in 2026
item.description: Dar es Salaam, Tanzania – The Council of East and Central Africa Football Associations (CECAFA) has announced a busy 2026 football calendar for the region. Tanzania will play a central role by hosting...

item.title: Weyburn hosts Canada’s last KFC all-day buffet
item.description: Weyburn, a small town in Saskatchewan, has gained national attention for hosting the last KFC All-Day Buffet in Canada. Since 1988, Larie Semen, the manager of Weyburn KFC, introduced this buffet opti...

item.title: The Human Cost of Modern Conflicts on Women and Children
item.description: The early 21st century has been marked by numerous high-stakes conflicts, with civilian populations, especially women and children, bearing the brunt. In Ukraine, the Russian invasion since February 2...

item.title: Three gold buyers shot dead in Cagayan over P1.2 million transaction
item.description: Three individuals from a Muslim community in Tuguegarao, identified as Salic, Mutalib, and Janisah Amerol, were fatally shot in Lal-lo, Cagayan during a gold purchase transaction. The victims had trav...

item.title: Understanding the Politics and Social Layers Behind Indian Appearance
item.description: In 'Stories We Wear: Status, Spectacle and the Politics of Appearance', journalist Shefalee Vasudev explores how Indians use appearance—from clothing to coffee habits—to signal social status, gender i...

item.title: Debate for California Governor Faces Criticism Over Exclusion of Candidates of Color
item.description: Former U.S. Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra and other candidates of color criticized the University of Southern California (USC) and ABC7 Los Angeles for excluding all candidates of...

item.title: 8-Year-Old Filipino Artist Wins Asia-Pacific Poster of the Year Award
item.description: Eight-year-old Alexander Maverick Racca Malunes, also known in the art world as BAP-K! (Batang Artist Pang-Kalawakan), has captured international attention for his imaginative artwork. On March 8, 202...

item.title: Manchester United Come Back to Defeat 10-Man Crystal Palace 2-1 and Climb to Third in Premier League
item.description: Manchester United turned the game around to secure a vital 2-1 victory over Crystal Palace at Old Trafford, moving them up to third position in the Premier League table. The visitors took an early lea...

item.title: AI and Hardware Shortages Challenge the Gaming Industry
item.description: The gaming industry is facing major disruptions due to the rapid rise of artificial intelligence and a global shortage of RAM, which is being consumed by AI-focused data centers. This shortage, dubbed...
